description: AAV capsid packaging-fitness regression on the FLIP-AAV benchmark. Task: predict the fitness of held-out capsid VP1 variants. NON-circular (label = the wet-lab packaging-fitness measurement, not a predictor).

ground_truth.citation: Dallago et al. 2021 (NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks); built on Bryant et al. 2021 Nat Biotechnol
ground_truth.source: github.com/J-SNACKKB/FLIP splits/aav (full data 217 MB/split, stays on the VM)

model: models/capsid_fitness.pkl (windowed one-hot VP1 555-595 HistGradientBoostingRegressor; gitignored + VM-mounted + regenerated via scripts/build_capsid_fitness.py, like position_effect.pkl)
baseline: mutation burden (negative Hamming from the train-set consensus window)
metric: Spearman correlation on held-out test; bootstrap (300) CI on learned-minus-baseline gap
gate: D-G1: learned beats the mutation-burden baseline on held-out FLIP-AAV (CI excludes 0) on >=1 split -> PASSED on both.
honesty: predicted capsid-fitness is a CANDIDATE for the MEASURED packaging axis; in-vivo human tropism is a known-unknown (the serotype->tissue priors come from approved therapies, not this model).
